title: AI capability control
body: In the field of artificial intelligence (AI) design, AI capability control proposals, also referred to as AI confinement, aim to increase human ability to monitor and control the behavior of AI systems, including proposed artificial general intelligences (AGIs), in order to reduce dangers they might pose if misaligned. Capability control becomes less effective as agents become more intelligent and their ability to exploit flaws in human control systems increases, potentially resulting in an existential risk from AI. Therefore, the Oxford philosopher Nick Bostrom and others recommend capability control methods only as a supplement to alignment methods.
